search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae dysuria telehealth clinical guideline PDF
site:doh.gov.ae dysuria urinary tract infection guideline UAE
site:dha.gov.ae virtual management dysuria red flags urinalysis culture
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Antibiotic selection was outside documentation scope.
population_applicability: Partial: age, sex, pregnancy, recurrence, diabetes, immunosuppression, and complicated-infection qualifiers are not fully encoded in the legacy workflow.
setting_applicability: Partial: exact for Dubai telehealth history, prompts, and selected investigation documentation, not physical examination, diagnosis, or treatment.
UAE_applicability: Direct Dubai telehealth guidance with local applicability and referral qualifiers retained.
recency_verification: The official current DHA issue 2 PDF was reviewed on 2026-07-13 and remains within its revision cycle.
superseded_check: No newer official DHA issue was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped dysuria presentation identity within qualified DHA telehealth scope.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines DHA-licensed telehealth dysuria scope and qualified applicability.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped urinary symptom, duration, fever, flank pain, vomiting, haematuria, pregnancy, and history-draft fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports onset, persistence, severity, duration, location, fever, rigors, nausea, vomiting, flank pain, medicine or procedure context, pregnancy and sexual context, trauma or irritants, haematuria, frequency, urgency, and voiding symptoms.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped red-flag prompts and flank or CVA tenderness documentation without automatic urgency.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports fever with flank pain or tenderness, vomiting, visible haematuria, pregnancy, diabetes, immunosuppression, and recurrence as prompts only, without automatic urgency or referral output.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped urinalysis, qualified culture, and qualified renal-function review documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports urinalysis, recommendation-qualified urine culture, and renal-function or electrolyte review in qualified pyelonephritis, nausea, or vomiting contexts.

unresolved_source_gaps: The workflow does not encode all age, sex, pregnancy, recurrence, comorbidity, telehealth, and complicated-infection qualifiers.

Urinary-retention prompts, recurrent UTI in a male, broad blood tests, CBC, CRP, imaging, most physical examination fields, hydration advice, medicine plan, recurrent-UTI plan, generic clinician plan, safety-net wording, follow-up, fixed intervals, and PRN wording remain unsupported.
Diagnosis, infection classification, antibiotic or other treatment, medicine dose, imaging, referral urgency, and follow-up timing remain clinician responsibilities.
